SQLite's write lock is acquired before any reads, so no two workers can read the same candidate state. No flock, no server, no daemon.\n315\t\n316\t```\n317\tWorker 1: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT candidates → score → UPDATE winner → COMMIT\n318\tWorker 2: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked at write lock until Worker 1 COMMITs) → SELECT → score → UPDATE → COMMIT\n319\tWorker 3: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked) → ...\n320\t...\n321\tWorker 20: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked) → SELECT → NONE (queue empty) → COMMIT (no-op)\n322\t```\n323\t\n324\tEach claim is a single CLI invocation. The critical section (lock held) is: SELECT scoring fields → score in memory → UPDATE winner + stale resets → COMMIT. Lock held for ~0.5-2ms.\n325\t\n326\t**Throughput**: SQLite handles claims purely in memory after the initial page cache warm-up:\n327\t\n328\t| Workspace | Active beads | Claim latency | Throughput |\n329\t|-----------|-------------|---------------|-----------|\n330\t| Typical (FORGE) | 248 | ~0.5ms | ~2000/sec |\n331\t| Medium (NEEDLE) | 518 | ~1ms | ~1000/sec |\n332\t| Large | 2000 | ~2ms | ~500/sec |\n333\t\n334\tWith 20 workers claiming simultaneously, the last worker waits ~20-40ms (19 queued transactions × ~1-2ms each). The fleet needs ~1-2 claims/sec at steady state — SQLite is overkill by 3 orders of magnitude.\n335\t\n336\t### 4.2 Atomic Claim (`src/claim.rs`)\n337\t\n338\tThe entire claim sequence runs inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. SQLite acquires the write lock before any reads, so no two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n339\t\n340\t```rust\n341\tfn claim(storage: &Storage, worker: &str, claim_ttl: Duration) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {\n342\t    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n343\t        let now = Utc::now();\n344\t\n345\t        // 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n346\t        tx.execute(\n347\t            \"UPDATE issues SET status='open', assignee=NULL, updated_at=?1\n348\t             WHERE status='in_progress' AND updated_at < ?2\",\n349\t            params![now, now - claim_ttl],\n350\t        )?;\n351\t\n352\t        // 2. Load scoring fields for open, unblocked candidates\n353\t        //    (blocked_issues_cache is a materialized view maintained on dep changes)\n354\t        let candidates: Vec<ScoreEntry> = tx.query_map(\n355\t            \"SELECT id, priority, updated_at,\n356\t                    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dependencies d\n357\t                     JOIN issues b ON b.id = d.depends_on_id\n358\t                     WHERE d.issue_id IN (\n359\t                         SELECT issue_id FROM dependencies\n360\t                         WHERE depends_on_id = issues.id AND type='blocks'\n361\t                     ) AND b.status = 'open') AS downstream_impact\n362\t             FROM issues\n363\t             WHERE status = 'open'\n364\t               AND id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id FROM blocked_issues_cache)\n365\t             ORDER BY downstream_impact DESC, priority ASC, created_at ASC\",\n366\t            [],\n367\t            |row| ScoreEntry::from_row(row),\n368\t        )?;\n369\t\n370\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n371\t            return Ok(None);\n372\t        }\n373\t\n374\t        // 3. Claim the top candidate\n375\t        let winner = &candidates[0];\n376\t        tx.execute(\n377\t            \"UPDATE issues SET status='in_progress', assignee=?1, updated_at=?2\n378\t             WHERE id=?3\",\n379\t            params![worker, now, winner.id],\n380\t        )?;\n381\t        tx.execute(\n382\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, old_value, new_value, created_at)\n383\t             VALUES (?1, 'claimed', ?2, 'open', 'in_progress', ?3)\",\n384\t            params![winner.id, worker, now],\n385\t        )?;\n386\t        tx.mark_dirty(&winner.id)?;\n387\t\n388\t        Ok(Some(tx.get_issue(&winner.id)?))\n389\t    })\n390\t}\n391\t```\n392\t\n393\t**Key properties**:\n394\t- **Correctness**: `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` holds the SQLite write lock for the entire read-score-update sequence. No two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n395\t- **Crash safety**: If the process crashes mid-transaction, SQLite rolls back automatically. No partial state is committed.\n396\t- **Stale reclamation is a side effect**: The first statement in every claim resets expired `in_progress` beads before scoring. No separate process.\n397\t- **Impact-weighted scoring**: The query orders by `downstream_impact` (beads that would unblock the most work) then priority, then FIFO.\n398\t- **No flock, no file I/O**: The entire claim is in-memory SQL execution after page cache warm-up. `issues.jsonl` is not touched.\n399\t\n400\t### 4.3 Multi-Workspace Claiming (`src/claim.rs`)\n401\t\n402\tFor `bf claim --any` (claim from any workspace), open each workspace's SQLite, run the scoring query on each, pick the global winner, claim it:\n403\t\n404\t```rust\n405\tfn claim_any(workspace_paths: &[PathBuf], worker: &str, claim_ttl: Duration) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {\n406\t    // Score across all workspaces — each Storage::open is cheap (WAL, page cache)\n407\t    let mut best: Option<(Score, PathBuf)> = None;\n408\t    for path in workspace_paths {\n409\t        let storage = Storage::open(&path.join(\".beads/beads.db\"))?;\n410\t        if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score(claim_ttl)? {\n411\t            if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n412\t                best = Some((score, path.clone()));\n413\t            }\n414\t        }\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    match best {\n418\t        None => Ok(None),\n419\t        Some((_, path)) => {\n420\t            let storage = Storage::open(&path.join(\".beads/beads.db\"))?;\n421\t            claim(&storage, worker, claim_ttl)\n422\t        }\n423\t    }\n424\t}\n425\t```\n426\t\n427\tNo cross-workspace locking needed — each `claim()` call uses `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` on its own SQLite file. Two workers racing on the same workspace are serialized by SQLite; two workers racing on different workspaces proceed in parallel.\n428\t\n429\t### 4.4 Stale Claim Detection\n430\t\n431\tNo background process needed. Stale detection runs as part of every `bf claim` invocation:\n432\t\n433\t1. Inside `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`: `UPDATE issues SET status='open' WHERE status='in_progress' AND updated_at < now - claim_ttl`\n434\t2. This runs before candidate scoring — stale beads become eligible again in the same transaction\n435\t3. Single SQL statement, no extra I/O\n436\t\n437\t**Claim TTL**: Default 30 minutes, configurable in `.beads/config.yaml`:\n438\t\n439\t```yaml\n440\tclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n441\t```\n442\t\n443\t**TTL tuning**: With 20 workers and tasks taking 5-15 minutes, 30 minutes is conservative. A worker that's actively working but slow will have its bead remain `in_progress` — stale reclamation only triggers if the worker hasn't updated the bead in 30 minutes. Since the worker closes the bead when done (writing a new entry), active workers never get reclaimed.\n444\t\n445\t**Manual stale scan**: For workspaces with no active claiming (no fleet running), `bf doctor --reclaim-stale` performs the same scan without claiming a bead. Can be run via cron for long-running workspaces with occasional stuck claims.\n446\t\n447\t### 4.5 CLI Usage\n448\t\n449\t```bash"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"40d7e1cb-f691-4ed3-b34e-f1f123dc7c3b","uuid":"5962132f-3a53-4ac4-98b5-f59cb353687e","timestamp":"2026-05-01T13:55:14.936Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"\nEach command:\n1. Locate `.beads/` directory (walk up from CWD)\n2. Load config + metadata\n3. Open SQLite (`beads.db`), apply schema if new\n4. Execute: mutations are SQL writes; reads are SQL queries\n5. `bf sync` is the only command that touches `issues.jsonl` — flush exports it, import reads it\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4: Concurrent Claiming\n\n### 4.1 Architecture: SQLite `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`\n\nThe thundering herd problem — N workers racing to claim the same bead — is solved by running the entire read-score-update sequence inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. SQLite's write lock is acquired before any reads, so no two workers can read the same candidate state. No flock, no server, no daemon.\n\n```\nWorker 1: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT candidates → score → UPDATE winner → COMMIT\nWorker 2: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked at write lock until Worker 1 COMMITs) → SELECT → score → UPDATE → COMMIT\nWorker 3: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked) → ...\n...\nWorker 20: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked) → SELECT → NONE (queue empty) → COMMIT (no-op)\n```\n\nEach claim is a single CLI invocation. The critical section (lock held) is: SELECT scoring fields → score in memory → UPDATE winner + stale resets → COMMIT. Lock held for ~0.5-2ms.\n\n**Throughput**: SQLite handles claims purely in memory after the initial page cache warm-up:\n\n| Workspace | Active beads | Claim latency | Throughput |\n|-----------|-------------|---------------|-----------|\n| Typical (FORGE) | 248 | ~0.5ms | ~2000/sec |\n| Medium (NEEDLE) | 518 | ~1ms | ~1000/sec |\n| Large | 2000 | ~2ms | ~500/sec |\n\nWith 20 workers claiming simultaneously, the last worker waits ~20-40ms (19 queued transactions × ~1-2ms each). The fleet needs ~1-2 claims/sec at steady state — SQLite is overkill by 3 orders of magnitude.\n\n### 4.2 Atomic Claim (`src/claim.rs`)\n\nThe entire claim sequence runs inside a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. SQLite acquires the write lock before any reads, so no two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n\n```rust\nfn claim(storage: &Storage, worker: &str, claim_ttl: Duration) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues SET status='open', assignee=NULL, updated_at=?1\n             WHERE status='in_progress' AND updated_at < ?2\",\n            params![now, now - claim_ttl],\n        )?;\n\n        // 2. Load scoring fields for open, unblocked candidates\n        //    (blocked_issues_cache is a materialized view maintained on dep changes)\n        let candidates: Vec<ScoreEntry> = tx.query_map(\n            \"SELECT id, priority, updated_at,\n                    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dependencies d\n                     JOIN issues b ON b.id = d.depends_on_id\n                     WHERE d.issue_id IN (\n                         SELECT issue_id FROM dependencies\n                         WHERE depends_on_id = issues.id AND type='blocks'\n                     ) AND b.status = 'open') AS downstream_impact\n             FROM issues\n             WHERE status = 'open'\n               AND id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id FROM blocked_issues_cache)\n             ORDER BY downstream_impact DESC, priority ASC, created_at ASC\",\n            [],\n            |row| ScoreEntry::from_row(row),\n        )?;\n\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // 3. Claim the top candidate\n        let winner = &candidates[0];\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues SET status='in_progress', assignee=?1, updated_at=?2\n             WHERE id=?3\",\n            params![worker, now, winner.id],\n        )?;\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, old_value, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?1, 'claimed', ?2, 'open', 'in_progress', ?3)\",\n            params![winner.id, worker, now],\n        )?;\n        tx.mark_dirty(&winner.id)?;\n\n        Ok(Some(tx.get_issue(&winner.id)?))\n    })\n}\n```\n\n**Key properties**:\n- **Correctness**: `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` holds the SQLite write lock for the entire read-score-update sequence. No two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n- **Crash safety**: If the process crashes mid-transaction, SQLite rolls back automatically. No partial state is committed.\n- **Stale reclamation is a side effect**: The first statement in every claim resets expired `in_progress` beads before scoring. No separate process.\n- **Impact-weighted scoring**: The query orders by `downstream_impact` (beads that would unblock the most work) then priority, then FIFO.\n- **No flock, no file I/O**: The entire claim is in-memory SQL execution after page cache warm-up. `issues.jsonl` is not touched.\n\n### 4.3 Multi-Workspace Claiming (`src/claim.rs`)\n\nFor `bf claim --any` (claim from any workspace), open each workspace's SQLite, run the scoring query on each, pick the global winner, claim it:\n\n```rust\nfn claim_any(workspace_paths: &[PathBuf], worker: &str, claim_ttl: Duration) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {\n    // Score across all workspaces — each Storage::open is cheap (WAL, page cache)\n    let mut best: Option<(Score, PathBuf)> = None;\n    for path in workspace_paths {\n        let storage = Storage::open(&path.join(\".beads/beads.db\"))?;\n        if let Some(score) = storage.top_candidate_score(claim_ttl)? {\n            if best.as_ref().map(|(b, _)| score > *b).unwrap_or(true) {\n                best = Some((score, path.clone()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    match best {\n        None => Ok(None),\n        Some((_, path)) => {\n            let storage = Storage::open(&path.join(\".beads/beads.db\"))?;\n            claim(&storage, worker, claim_ttl)\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nNo cross-workspace locking needed — each `claim()` call uses `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` on its own SQLite file. Two workers racing on the same workspace are serialized by SQLite; two workers racing on different workspaces proceed in parallel.\n\n### 4.4 Stale Claim Detection\n\nNo background process needed. Stale detection runs as part of every `bf claim` invocation:\n\n1. Inside `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`: `UPDATE issues SET status='open' WHERE status='in_progress' AND updated_at < now - claim_ttl`\n2. This runs before candidate scoring — stale beads become eligible again in the same transaction\n3. Single SQL statement, no extra I/O\n\n**Claim TTL**: Default 30 minutes, configurable in `.beads/config.yaml`:\n\n```yaml\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n```\n\n**TTL tuning**: With 20 workers and tasks taking 5-15 minutes, 30 minutes is conservative. A worker that's actively working but slow will have its bead remain `in_progress` — stale reclamation only triggers if the worker hasn't updated the bead in 30 minutes. Since the worker closes the bead when done (writing a new entry), active workers never get reclaimed.\n\n**Manual stale scan**: For workspaces with no active claiming (no fleet running), `bf doctor --reclaim-stale` performs the same scan without claiming a bead. Can be run via cron for long-running workspaces with occasional stuck claims.\n\n### 4.5 CLI Usage\n\n```bash","numLines":150,"startLine":300,"totalLines":1560}}}

workspace root (contains .beads/)\n27\t///\n28\t/// # Returns\n29\t/// * `Ok(DoctorResult)` - Health check results\n30\tpub fn check(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<DoctorResult> {\n31\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n32\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n33\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n34\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n35\t    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n36\t\n37\t    let mut issues = Vec::new();\n38\t    let mut db_ok = true;\n39\t    let mut jsonl_ok = true;\n40\t\n41\t    // Check database\n42\t    let (db_issue_count, db_integrity_ok) = check_database(&db_path)?;\n43\t    if !db_integrity_ok {\n44\t        db_ok = false;\n45\t        issues.push(\"Database integrity check failed\".to_string());\n46\t    }\n47\t\n48\t    // Check JSONL file\n49\t    let (jsonl_line_count, jsonl_valid) = check_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n50\t    if          db_issue_count, jsonl_line_count\n61\t            ));\n62\t        }\n63\t    }\n64\t\n65\t    Ok(DoctorResult {\n66\t        db_ok,\n67\t        jsonl_ok,\n68\t   let conn = Connection::open(db_path)?;\n77\t\n78\t    // Apply schema if database is new (no tables yet)\n79\t    let needs_schema: bool = conn.query_row(\n80\t        \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='issues'\",\n81\t        [],\n82\t        |row| row.get::<_, i64>(0),\n83\t    ).map(|n| n == 0).unwrap_or(true);\n84\t    if needs_schema {\n85\t        crate::storage::schema::apply_schema(&conn)?;\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    // Run PRAGMA integrity_check\n89\t    let integrity_result: String = conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA integrity_check\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n90\t\n91\t    let integrity_ok = integrity_result == \"ok\";\n92\t\n93\t    // Count issues\n94\t    let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n95\t\n96\t    Ok((count as usize, integrity_ok))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Check JSONL file validity.\n100\tfn check_jsonl(jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<(usize, bool)> {\n101\t    if !jsonl_path.exists() {\n102\t        return Ok((0, true)); // Empty workspace is valid\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    let mut count = 0;\n106\t    let mut valid = true;\n107\t\n108\t    match stream_issues(jsonl_path) {\n109\t        Ok(iter) => {\n110\t            for result in iter {\n111\t                match result {\n112\t                    Ok(_) => count += 1,\n113\t                    Err(e) => {\n114\t                        valid = false;\n115\t                        eprintln!(\"Invalid JSONL line: {}\", e);\n116\t                    }\n117\t                }\n118\t            }\n119\t        }\n120\t        Err(e) => {\n121\t            return Err(anyhow!(\"Failed to read JSONL file: {}\", e));\n122\t        }\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    Ok((count, valid))\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t/// Check consistency between database and JSONL.\n129\t///\n130\t/// Returns Some(count) if counts differ, None if they match.\n131\tfn check_consistency(db_path: &Path, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<usize>> {\n132\t    let conn = Connection::open(db_path)?;\n133\t    let db_count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n134\t\n135\t    let jsonl_count = if jsonl_path.exists() {\n136\t        let iter = stream_issues(jsonl_path)?;\n137\t        iter.count() as i64\n138\t    } else {\n139\t        0\n140\t    };\n141\t\n142\t    if db_count != jsonl_count {\n143\t        Ok(Some(db_count as usize))\n144\t    } else {\n145\t        Ok(None)\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Repair the database by rebuilding from JSONL.\n150\t///\n151\t/// This is the recovery operation when the database is corrupted or missing.\n152\t/// The JSONL file is the authoritative source of truth.\n153\t///\n154\t/// # Arguments\n155\t/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n156\t///\n157\t/// # Returns\n158\t/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads imported\n159\tpub fn repair(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n160\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n161\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n162\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n163\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n164\t    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n165\t\n166\t    // Check if JSONL exists\n167\t    if !jsonl_path.exists() {\n168\t        return Err(anyhow!(\n169\t            \"Cannot repair: JSONL file not found at {}\",\n170\t            jsonl_path.display()\n171\t        ));\n172\t    }\n173\t\n174\t    // Backup existing database if it exists\n175\t    if db_path.exists() {\n176\t        let backup_path = db_path.with_extension(&format!(\n177\t            \"db.backup.{}\",\n178\t            chrono::Utc::now().format(\"%Y%m%d%H%M%S\")\n179\t        ));\n180\t        std::fs::copy(&db_path, &backup_path)?;\n181\t        eprintln!(\"Backed up existing database to {}\", backup_path.display());\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    // Delete old database\n185\t    if db_path.exists() {\n186\t        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    // Create new database and import JSONL\n190\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n191\t\n192\t    let result = import_jsonl(&jsonl_path, |issue| {\n193\t        storage.create_issue(issue)?;\n194\t        Ok(true)\n195\t    })?;\n196\t\n197\t    // Rebuild blocked cache\n198\t    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n199\t\n200\t    Ok(result.imported)\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t/// Rebuild the blocked issues cache.\n204\t///\n205\t/// This materialized view should be rebuilt after dependency or status changes.\n206\tpub fn rebuild_cache(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n207\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n208\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n209\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n210\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n211\t\n212\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n213\t    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n214\t\n215\t    Ok(())\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t/// Reclaim stale in_progress beads.\n219\t///\n220\t/// Resets beads that have been in_progress for longer than the TTL\n221\t/// back to open status.\n222\t///\n223\t/// # Arguments\n224\t/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n225\t/// * `ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes (default from config is 30)\n226\t///\n227\t/// # Returns\n228\t/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads reclaimed\n229\tpub fn reclaim_stale(workspace_dir: &Path, ttl_minutes: i64) -> Result<usize> {\n230\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n231\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n232\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n233\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n234\t\n235\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n236\t\n237\t    let reclaimed = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n238\t        let stale_cutoff = chrono::Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::minutes(ttl_minutes);\n239\t\n240\t        let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n241\t            \"UPDATE issues\n242\t             SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n243\t             WHERE status = 'in_progress' AND updated_at < ?\",\n244\t            rusqlite::params![chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n245\t        )?;\n246\t\n247\t        Ok::<_, anyhow::Error>(reclaimed)\n248\t    })?;\n249\t\n250\t    Ok(reclaimed)\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t/// Initialize a new database from an existing JSONL file.\n254\t///\n255\t/// Use this to create a fresh database from a JSONL export without\n256\t/// affecting the existing database.\n257\t///\n258\t/// # Arguments\n259\t/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n260\t/// * `jsonl_path` - Path to the JSONL file to import from\n261\t///\n262\t/// # Returns\n263\t/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads imported\n264\tpub fn init_from_jsonl(workspace_dir: &Path, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n265\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n266\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n267\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n268\t    let  let result = import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n282\t        storage.create_issue(issue)?;\n283\t        Ok(true)\n284\t    })?;\n285\t\n286\t    // Rebuild blocked cache\n287\t    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n288\t\n289\t    Ok(result.imported)\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t/// Verify database schema version.\n293\t///\n294\t/// Checks that all required tables and indexes exist.\n295\tpub fn verify_schema(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<bool> {\n296\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n297\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n298\t    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n299\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n300\t\n301\t    let conn = Connection::open(&db_path)?;\n302\t\n303\t    // Check for critical tables\n304\t    let tables = [\n305\t        \"issues\", \"dependencies\", \"labels\", \"comments\", \"events\",\n306\t        \"config\", \"metadata\", \"dirty_issues\", \"export_hashes\",\n307\t        \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"child_counters\",\n308\t    ];\n309\t\n310\t    for table in &tables {\n311\t        let )?;\n316\t\n317\t        if exists == 0 {\n318\t            eprintln!(\"Missing table: {}\", table);\n319\t            return Ok(false);\n320\t        }\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    Ok(true)\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t#[cfg(test)]\n327\tmod tests {\n328\t    use super::*;\n329\t    use crate::config::init_workspace;\n330\t    use crate::model::{Issue, Status, Priority, Doctor and repair operations for bead-forge.\n//!\n//! Provides health checking and recovery operations for bead databases,\n//! including corruption detection and JSONL-based repair.\n\nuse crate::config::{find_beads_dir, load_metadata};\nuse crate::jsonl::{import_jsonl, stream_issues};\nuse crate::storage::Storage;\nuse anyhow::{anyhow, Result};\nuse rusqlite::Connection;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\n/// Doctor check results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DoctorResult {\n    pub db_ok: bool,\n    pub jsonl_ok: bool,\n    pub jsonl_line_count: usize,\n    pub db_issue_count: usize,\n    pub issues: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Perform a health check on the bead database and JSONL file.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(DoctorResult)` - Health check results\npub fn check(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<DoctorResult> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n\n    let mut issues = Vec::new();\n    let mut db_ok = true;\n    let mut jsonl_ok = true;\n\n    // Check database\n    let (db_issue_count, db_integrity_ok) = check_database(&db_path)?;\n    if !db_integrity_ok {\n        db_ok = false;\n        issues.push(\"Database integrity check failed\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Check JSONL file\n    let (jsonl_line_count, jsonl_valid) = check_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n    if !jsonl_valid {\n        jsonl_ok = false;\n        issues.push(\"JSONL file contains invalid lines\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Check consistency\n    if db_ok && jsonl_ok {\n        if check_consistency(&db_path, &jsonl_path)?.is_some() {\n            issues.push(format!(\n                \"Count mismatch: database has {} issues, JSONL has {}\",\n                db_issue_count, jsonl_line_count\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(DoctorResult {\n        db_ok,\n        jsonl_ok,\n        jsonl_line_count,\n        db_issue_count,\n        issues,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Check database integrity.\nfn check_database(db_path: &Path) -> Result<(usize, bool)> {\n    let conn = Connection::open(db_path)?;\n\n    // Apply schema if database is new (no tables yet)\n    let needs_schema: bool = conn.query_row(\n        \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='issues'\",\n        [],\n        |row| row.get::<_, i64>(0),\n    ).map(|n| n == 0).unwrap_or(true);\n    if needs_schema {\n        crate::storage::schema::apply_schema(&conn)?;\n    }\n\n    // Run PRAGMA integrity_check\n    let integrity_result: String = conn.query_row(\"PRAGMA integrity_check\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n\n    let integrity_ok = integrity_result == \"ok\";\n\n    // Count issues\n    let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n\n    Ok((count as usize, integrity_ok))\n}\n\n/// Check JSONL file validity.\nfn check_jsonl(jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<(usize, bool)> {\n    if !jsonl_path.exists() {\n        return Ok((0, true)); // Empty workspace is valid\n    }\n\n    let mut count = 0;\n    let mut valid = true;\n\n    match stream_issues(jsonl_path) {\n        Ok(iter) => {\n            for result in iter {\n                match result {\n                    Ok(_) => count += 1,\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        valid = false;\n                        eprintln!(\"Invalid JSONL line: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            return Err(anyhow!(\"Failed to read JSONL file: {}\", e));\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok((count, valid))\n}\n\n/// Check consistency between database and JSONL.\n///\n/// Returns Some(count) if counts differ, None if they match.\nfn check_consistency(db_path: &Path, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<usize>> {\n    let conn = Connection::open(db_path)?;\n    let db_count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n\n    let jsonl_count = if jsonl_path.exists() {\n        let iter = stream_issues(jsonl_path)?;\n        iter.count() as i64\n    } else {\n        0\n    };\n\n    if db_count != jsonl_count {\n        Ok(Some(db_count as usize))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Repair the database by rebuilding from JSONL.\n///\n/// This is the recovery operation when the database is corrupted or missing.\n/// The JSONL file is the authoritative source of truth.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads imported\npub fn repair(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n\n    // Check if JSONL exists\n    if !jsonl_path.exists() {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\n            \"Cannot repair: JSONL file not found at {}\",\n            jsonl_path.display()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Backup existing database if it exists\n    if db_path.exists() {\n        let backup_path = db_path.with_extension(&format!(\n            \"db.backup.{}\",\n            chrono::Utc::now().format(\"%Y%m%d%H%M%S\")\n        ));\n        std::fs::copy(&db_path, &backup_path)?;\n        eprintln!(\"Backed up existing database to {}\", backup_path.display());\n    }\n\n    // Delete old database\n    if db_path.exists() {\n        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n    }\n\n    // Create new database and import JSONL\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let result = import_jsonl(&jsonl_path, |issue| {\n        storage.create_issue(issue)?;\n        Ok(true)\n    })?;\n\n    // Rebuild blocked cache\n    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n\n    Ok(result.imported)\n}\n\n/// Rebuild the blocked issues cache.\n///\n/// This materialized view should be rebuilt after dependency or status changes.\npub fn rebuild_cache(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Reclaim stale in_progress beads.\n///\n/// Resets beads that have been in_progress for longer than the TTL\n/// back to open status.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n/// * `ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes (default from config is 30)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads reclaimed\npub fn reclaim_stale(workspace_dir: &Path, ttl_minutes: i64) -> Result<usize> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let reclaimed = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        let stale_cutoff = chrono::Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::minutes(ttl_minutes);\n\n        let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE status = 'in_progress' AND updated_at < ?\",\n            rusqlite::params![chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok::<_, anyhow::Error>(reclaimed)\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(reclaimed)\n}\n\n/// Initialize a new database from an existing JSONL file.\n///\n/// Use this to create a fresh database from a JSONL export without\n/// affecting the existing database.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `workspace_dir` - Path to the workspace root (contains .beads/)\n/// * `jsonl_path` - Path to the JSONL file to import from\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(usize)` - Number of beads imported\npub fn init_from_jsonl(workspace_dir: &Path, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<usize> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    // Check if JSONL exists\n    if !jsonl_path.exists() {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\n            \"JSONL file not found at {}\",\n            jsonl_path.display()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Create new database and import JSONL\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let result = import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n        storage.create_issue(issue)?;\n        Ok(true)\n    })?;\n\n    // Rebuild blocked cache\n    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache()?;\n\n    Ok(result.imported)\n}\n\n/// Verify database schema version.\n///\n/// Checks that all required tables and indexes exist.\npub fn verify_schema(workspace_dir: &Path) -> Result<bool> {\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(workspace_dir)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {}\", workspace_dir.display()))?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    let conn = Connection::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    // Check for critical tables\n    let tables = [\n        \"issues\", \"dependencies\", \"labels\", \"comments\", \"events\",\n        \"config\", \"metadata\", \"dirty_issues\", \"export_hashes\",\n        \"blocked_issues_cache\", \"child_counters\",\n    ];\n\n    for table in &tables {\n        let exists: i64 = conn.query_row(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?1\",\n            &[table],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        if exists == 0 {\n            eprintln!(\"Missing table: {}\", table);\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(true)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::config::init_workspace;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType};\n    use crate::jsonl::export_jsonl;\n    use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_check_empty_workspace() {\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let workspace = temp_dir.path();\n        let beads_dir = workspace.join(\".beads\");\n\n        init_workspace(&beads_dir, \"bf\").unwrap();\n\n        let result = check(workspace).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.db_ok);\n        assert!(result.jsonl_ok);\n        assert_eq!(result.db_issue_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.jsonl_line_count, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_repair_from_jsonl() {\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let workspace = temp_dir.path();\n        let beads_dir = workspace.join(\".beads\");\n\n        init_workspace(&beads_dir, \"bf\").unwrap();\n        let metadata = load_metadata(&beads_dir).unwrap();\n        let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n        let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n\n        // Create initial database and export to JSONL\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path).unwrap();\n        let issue = Issue {\n            id: \"bf-test\".to_string(),\n            title: \"Test\".to_string(),\n            status: Status::Open,\n            priority: Priority::MEDIUM,\n            issue_type: IssueType::Task,\n            source_repo: Some(\".\".to_string()),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        export_jsonl(&jsonl_path, || storage.list_all_issues()).unwrap();\n\n        // Delete database\n        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path).unwrap();\n\n        // Repair from JSONL\n        let imported = repair(workspace).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(imported, 1);\n\n        // Verify repaired database\n Subcommands mirror br exactly.\n254\t\n255\t```\n256\tbf create    --title \"...\" --type <type> --priority <N> [--description \"...\"] [--label ...]\n257\tbf list      [--status <s>] [--type <t>] [--assignee <a>] [--limit N] [--format json|text|toon]\n258\tbf show      <id>\n259\tbf update    <id> [--status <s>] [--assignee <a>] [--priority <N>] [--title \"...\"] ...\n260\tbf close     <id> [--reason \"...\"]\n261\tbf reopen    <id>\n262\tbf delete    <id>\n263\tbf ready         [--limit N]                     # unblocked, open, sorted by priority\n264\tbf claim         --assignee <id>                 # atomic claim (see Phase 4)\n265\t                 [--model <model>]               # e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6\n266\t                 [--harness <name>]              # e.g. needle\n267\t                 [--harness-version <ver>]       # e.g. 0.5.2\n268\t                 [--workspace <path>] [--any]\n269\tbf critical-path <epic-id>                       # show critical path + float for each bead\n270\tbf dep           add-blocker <parent> <child>\n271\tbf dep           remove-blocker <parent> <child>\n272\tbf dep           tree <id>\n273\tbf label         <id> add <label>\n274\tbf label         <id> remove <label>\n275\tbf comment       <id> [--author <a>] [--text \"...\"]\n276\tbf sync          --flush-only\n277\tbf sync          --import-only\n278\tbf doctor        [--repair]\n279\tbf migrate       [<workspace>]                  # Binary name: `bf`.
